brief.headline_en: UPC Evolves: PMAC Launches, Case Law Tightens Infringement Standards
brief.summary_zh: 2026年上半年，统一专利法院（UPC）加速完善PMAC调解机制并上线案件系统，推动ADR成为低成本解纷新路径。司法层面，杜塞尔多夫等分院在等同原则、功能性限定及临时禁令举证上确立严格标准，同时上诉庭强化程序合规审查。企业需警惕单一零部件侵权风险，优化权利要求布局，并利用撤诉规则灵活应对诉讼策略。
key_points: PMAC程序规则获批且系统上线，调解仲裁覆盖单一专利及SEP/FRAND纠纷，提供高效替代性争议解决渠道。
司法判例趋严：等同判定具累积性，功能性限定扩大保护范围至单独部件，临时禁令需证明单一产品特征完整实现。
程序合规红线明确：上诉期自附理由判决日起算，逾期人为错误可救济但需严格核查，登录系统不视为应诉管辖。
actions: 优先利用PMAC及EUIPO调解机制处理跨境IP争议，降低维权成本；在合同中预设调解条款以掌握主动权。
全面排查对欧出口硬件的专利风险，避免使用易引发直接侵权的功能性限定语言，优化权利要求布局。
建立严格的UPC诉讼期限双重核查机制，善用“可原谅错误”条款申请救济，并谨慎管理官网涉诉声明以防反制。
watchlist: 关注UPC主要分部早期判例对欧洲专利法解释趋势的影响，预判法律确定性变化。
监测PMAC在SEP/FRAND纠纷中的实际裁决案例，评估其对许可谈判格局的重塑作用。
追踪成员国公约加入状态对单一专利效力的潜在影响，动态调整传统验证与单一专利的组合策略。
brief.summary_en: In the first half of 2026, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) significantly advanced its alternative dispute resolution framework by approving procedural rules for the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) and launching its online case management system. This development establishes mediation and arbitration as efficient, lower-cost pathways for resolving Unitary Patent and SEP/FRAND disputes. Concurrently, judicial practice has hardened. Local Divisions in Düsseldorf and Hamburg have established strict standards for equivalence, functional claim limitations, and preliminary injunctions, requiring proof that all technical features are embodied within a single product. items.title: Unified Patent Court published the 2025 annual report
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院发布2025年度报告
items.title_en: UPC Releases 2025 Annual Report Highlighting Three-Year Progress and New Case Management System
items.summary: The UPC published its second annual report
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）发布第二份年度报告，回顾其成立三年来的活动、案件量及关键成就。报告强调新案件管理系统（CMS）的成功上线是重要里程碑，体现了与欧洲专利局及用户组织的紧密合作。法院高层指出，UPC已巩固其作为领先知识产权诉讼论坛的地位，致力于在技术快速进步背景下提供高质量、及时的判决，并持续加强判例法发展及利益相关方沟通。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has published its second annual report, reviewing activities, caseload, and key achievements since its establishment three years ago. The report highlights the successful launch of the new Case Management System as a major milestone, demonstrating effective cooperation between the UPC, the European Patent Office, and user organizations. Court leadership emphasized that the UPC has solidified its position as a leading IP litigation forum. The institution remains committed to developing consistent case law, engaging with stakeholders, and delivering high-quality decisions within competitive timeframes. These efforts aim to sustain growth and efficiency amidst rapid technological progress, ensuring the Court is well-equipped to handle future challenges in the evolving European patent landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: UPC运作日趋成熟，诉讼效率与透明度提升将增加其在欧专利纠纷中的吸引力。中国企业应密切关注其判例法发展及新系统对程序的影响，优化在欧洲的专利布局与维权策略，利用其高效机制应对潜在侵权风险或发起进攻性诉讼。
items.ai_insight_en: As the UPC matures, its increasing procedural efficiency and transparency enhance its attractiveness for resolving European patent disputes. Rights holders should closely monitor the development of UPC case law to understand judicial trends and predict outcomes. Adapting to the streamlined procedures offered by the new Case Management System allows for optimized litigation strategies across member states. Companies can leverage these transparent mechanisms to proactively manage patent risks and assert enforcement rights effectively. This environment supports both defensive risk mitigation and offensive enforcement actions, requiring updated approaches to European patent portfolio management and dispute resolution planning.

items.title: PMAC’s procedural rules adopted by the Administrative Committee
items.title_zh: UPC行政委员会批准PMAC程序规则
items.title_en: UPC Administrative Committee Adopts PMAC Procedural Rules for Alternative Dispute Resolution
items.summary: The procedural rules of the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) were formally approved and adopted by the Administrative Committee on the 24 April 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月24日，统一专利法院（UPC）行政委员会正式批准专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）的程序规则。PMAC将提供调解、仲裁及专家裁定等争议解决服务，涵盖欧洲专利、单一专利及相关跨境纠纷，包括SEP/FRAND案件。预计5月启动调解服务，年内陆续开放其他服务。此举标志着UPC体系下替代性争议解决机制的正式确立，为权利人提供了诉讼之外的多元化纠纷处理路径。
items.ai_summary_en: On 24 April 2026, the Administrative Committee of the Unified Patent Court formally approved and adopted the procedural rules governing the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre. This development establishes a structured framework for alternative dispute resolution within the UPC system. The Centre will provide mediation, arbitration, expert determination, and hybrid processes to resolve disputes concerning European and Unitary Patents. Its scope includes complex cross-border conflicts and standard-essential patent licensing negotiations under FRAND terms. Mediation services are scheduled to commence in May 2026, with arbitration and expert determination services following later in the year. The full text of the rules and associated fee schedules are publicly available on the official websites of both the Centre and the Unified Patent Court.
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants facing patent litigation risks in Europe should evaluate the newly established non-litigation pathways offered by the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre. Compared to traditional UPC proceedings, mediation and arbitration provide greater confidentiality and potentially more predictable cost structures. These mechanisms are particularly relevant for resolving stalemates in complex standard-essential patent licensing negotiations. Rights holders and alleged infringers should familiarize themselves with the adopted procedural rules and fee schedules. Integrating these alternative dispute resolution options into broader IP enforcement strategies allows for flexible management of cross-border intellectual property conflicts without immediate recourse to formal court judgments.

items.title: PMAC's Case Management System now live
items.title_zh: PMAC案件管理系统正式上线
items.title_en: UPC Patent Mediation Centre Launches Online Case Management System for ADR Proceedings
items.summary: As of today, 12 May 2026, the Case Management System of the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) is live
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月12日，统一专利法院（UPC）宣布其专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）的案件管理系统（CCMS）正式启用。该系统支持用户在线提交替代性争议解决（ADR）信息及调解程序请求。PMAC网站已设立专门页面提供系统使用指南。此举标志着UPC在优化专利纠纷非诉讼解决机制方面迈出重要一步，为当事人提供了更便捷的调解与仲裁服务渠道，有助于提高争议解决效率并降低诉讼成本。
items.ai_summary_en: On 12 May 2026, the Unified Patent Court announced that the Case Management System of its Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre is now fully operational. This digital platform enables parties to submit requests for Alternative Dispute Resolution information sessions and mediation proceedings directly online. The launch represents a significant step in optimizing non-litigious mechanisms for resolving patent disputes within the UPC framework. By providing a dedicated interface for these services, the Centre aims to offer rights holders a more accessible and efficient channel for settlement. This development is expected to enhance the overall efficiency of dispute resolution while helping participants manage and potentially reduce the high costs associated with traditional cross-border patent litigation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇专利纠纷时，应关注并利用PMAC的调解与仲裁机制。相比UPC诉讼，ADR程序通常更具成本效益且保密性强。建议企业熟悉CCMS操作流程，在争议初期评估通过调解快速和解的可能性，以规避高昂的跨国诉讼风险并维护商业关系。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants facing patent conflicts in Europe should evaluate the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre’s services as a strategic alternative to full litigation. ADR procedures generally offer greater cost efficiency and confidentiality compared to standard UPC court proceedings. Rights holders are advised to familiarize themselves with the new Case Management System to facilitate rapid initiation of these processes. Assessing the potential for early settlement through mediation can help mitigate the financial risks of prolonged litigation and preserve valuable commercial relationships during disputes.

items.title: LD Helsinki, April 29, 2026, Decision On Infringement And Validity, UPC_CFI_214/2023, UPC_CFI_403/2025
items.title_zh: UPC赫尔辛基分院：专利侵权与有效性裁决及等同原则适用
items.title_en: UPC Helsinki Adopts Hague Equivalence Approach and Limits Revocation Review Scope
items.summary: The Helsinki Local Division of the Unified Patent Court examined infringement proceedings involving EP 3 295 663, with AIM Sport Development AG asserting patent rights against multiple TGI Sport entities across European jurisdictions. The case included a counterclaim for revocation, requiring the court to analyze validity under Articles 54, 56, and 123(2) EPC alongside infringement claims under Article 69 EPC.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）赫尔辛基地方分院就EP 3 295 663号专利作出侵权与有效性判决。在缺乏上诉法院先例时，法院采纳海牙分院的等同侵权判定路径，强调等同标准需累积满足，任一不成立即无侵权。针对撤销反诉，法院指出若当事人未充分论证“新增事项”，仅需回应已 substantiated 的部分。该案明确了UPC内部司法一致性的重要性及举证责任边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Helsinki Local Division issued a decision on infringement and validity concerning EP 3 295 663. In proceedings brought by AIM Sport Development AG against multiple TGI Sport entities, the court addressed key procedural and substantive issues. Absent Court of Appeal precedent on equivalence, the Division adopted the approach established by The Hague Local Division to ensure legal certainty. The judgment clarified that equivalence criteria are cumulative; failure to meet any single criterion precludes a finding of infringement. Regarding the counterclaim for revocation, the court ruled it would only address allegations supported by substantiated arguments. Since the defendants provided minimal reasoning for their added matter claim, the court’s analysis was correspondingly limited, highlighting strict boundaries for party submissions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需密切关注各地方分院对等同原则的司法倾向，尤其是海牙、赫尔辛基等关键分院。在提出无效抗辩时，必须提供详尽的技术比对与法律论证，避免仅凭形式主张导致被驳回。建议建立跨法域的专利风险预警机制，统一应诉策略以应对UPC日益增强的判例约束力。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the importance of judicial consistency among UPC Local Divisions, particularly regarding the doctrine of equivalents. Practitioners should note that while divisions may look to peers like The Hague for guidance, the cumulative nature of equivalence tests offers potential defense avenues. Furthermore, the ruling on Article 123(2) EPC emphasizes that formalistic invalidity claims lacking detailed technical or legal substantiation will receive limited judicial attention. Parties must ensure robust, evidence-based arguments in counterclaims to secure comprehensive court consideration, avoiding reliance on bare assertions that may be dismissed without full analysis.

items.title: Where Should Cases Go? Or Where Should Patent Law Be Made?
items.title_zh: 案件去向与法律制定：统一专利法院案件分配的制度反思
items.title_en: Case Distribution and Lawmaking: Institutional Reflections on UPC Case Allocation
items.summary: "Gedanken ohne Inhalt sind leer, Anschauungen ohne Begriffe sind blind." — Immanuel Kant (Thoughts without content are empty, intuitions without concepts are blind.)
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文回应关于统一专利法院（UPC）案件分配过于抽象的批评，指出问题核心在于案件集中是否扭曲了UPC法的形成条件。尽管案件集中于特定分部反映了用户信心与可预测性，但法院并非市场，其产出是法律。若法律实践仅由少数中心通过重复程序塑造，将导致早期教义固化。作者主张需审视这种由用户偏好驱动的自强化均衡，因其可能非中性地决定未来专利法的结构与发展路径。
items.ai_summary_en: This article addresses criticisms that debates on Unified Patent Court (UPC) case distribution are overly abstract. It argues the core issue is whether case concentration distorts the conditions under which UPC law is formed. While concentration in specific divisions reflects user confidence and predictability, courts produce law rather than merely allocating market demand. If legal practice is shaped exclusively by a few centers through repetitive procedures, early doctrines risk becoming entrenched. The author contends that this self-reinforcing equilibrium, driven by user preferences, requires scrutiny as it may non-neutrally determine the future structure and development path of patent law within the UPC system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应密切关注UPC主要分部的司法倾向，因早期判例将深远影响后续诉讼策略。建议避免盲目追随热门法庭，需综合评估各分部的程序效率与文化差异，以规避因‘胜者通吃’效应导致的法律解释单一化风险，优化全球专利布局与应诉策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners should closely monitor judicial tendencies in major UPC divisions, as early precedents will significantly influence subsequent litigation strategies. Rather than blindly following popular forums, parties must comprehensively assess procedural efficiency and cultural differences across divisions. This approach helps mitigate risks associated with the homogenization of legal interpretation caused by winner-takes-all effects. Strategic diversification of forum selection can optimize global patent layouts and defense postures, ensuring resilience against potential doctrinal rigidity emerging from concentrated case loads.

items.title_zh: UPC汉堡地院：辅助请求数量及权利要求宽泛解释影响新颖性
items.title_en: UPC Hamburg: High Auxiliary Requests Accepted; Broad Claim Interpretation Destroys Novelty
items.summary: The Local Division Hamburg of the Unified Patent Court examined infringement and revocation claims involving EP 2 077 811, a patent held by Teleflex Life Sciences II LLC and challenged by Speed Care Mineral GmbH. The proceedings addressed questions of patent validity under multiple European Patent Convention articles and the application of the Rules of Procedure in cross-border patent disputes across ten European jurisdictions.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）汉堡地方法院审理了Teleflex诉Speed Care案，涉及EP 2 077 811专利的侵权与无效。法院裁定在面临多重有效性挑战时，多达51项的辅助请求可被允许；但被告若已知理由却延迟提出反诉，将因缺乏勤勉而被驳回。此外，法院对“粘土材料”等术语进行宽泛的功能性解释，虽利于认定侵权，但也扩大了现有技术范围，导致专利新颖性丧失。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Local Division in Hamburg adjudicated infringement and revocation actions concerning EP 2 077 811, held by Teleflex Life Sciences II LLC against Speed Care Mineral GmbH. The Court permitted up to 51 auxiliary requests due to the complexity of multiple validity challenges. However, it rejected a late amendment to extend the counterclaim for revocation, citing the defendant’s lack of reasonable diligence under Rule 263(2)(a) RoP, as grounds were known months prior. Crucially, the Court applied a broad functional interpretation to terms like “clay material” and “binder.” While this approach potentially favored an infringement finding, it simultaneously expanded the scope of relevant prior art, ultimately leading to a finding that the patent lacked novelty.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，应注重程序策略的严谨性，避免以已知理由延迟抗辩。同时，需警惕权利要求宽泛解释带来的风险，在撰写和修改权利要求时平衡保护范围与新颖性，避免因功能性限定过宽而引入不利现有技术，确保专利稳定性。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights critical procedural and substantive risks in UPC litigation. Parties must strictly adhere to procedural timelines and demonstrate reasonable diligence when raising validity defenses; delaying known arguments can lead to their exclusion. Substantively, while broad functional claim language may seem advantageous for establishing infringement, it carries significant risk. Such interpretations can inadvertently expand the pool of applicable prior art, undermining novelty. Practitioners should carefully balance claim breadth with stability, ensuring that functional definitions do not introduce unexpected anticipatory disclosures during validity assessments.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["Numerous auxiliary requests may be admissible when facing diverse and complex validity attacks regarding novelty and inventive step.", "Late amendments extending revocation counterclaims are rejected if the defendant lacks reasonable diligence under procedural rules.", "Broad functional claim interpretations can widen the prior art base, potentially destroying patent novelty despite aiding infringement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC诉讼需严守程序时限，避免以已知理由延迟抗辩", "平衡权利要求范围与新颖性，警惕宽泛解释引入现有技术"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strict adherence to procedural deadlines is essential; delaying known validity arguments risks exclusion from proceedings.", "Practice note: Balance claim breadth with novelty stability; avoid overly broad functional definitions that may expand prior art scope.", "What to monitor: How courts interpret functional claim terms and their impact on the admissibility of prior art in validity challenges."]
core_points_en: Numerous auxiliary requests may be admissible when facing diverse and complex validity attacks regarding novelty and inventive step.
Late amendments extending revocation counterclaims are rejected if the defendant lacks reasonable diligence under procedural rules.
Broad functional claim interpretations can widen the prior art base, potentially destroying patent novelty despite aiding infringement.

Practice note: Balance claim breadth with novelty stability; avoid overly broad functional definitions that may expand prior art scope.
What to monitor: How courts interpret functional claim terms and their impact on the admissibility of prior art in validity challenges.

items.title: Unified Patent Court Insights: Claim Construction
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院：打印机供墨容器权利要求解释与侵权判定
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Functional 'Mountable' Language Defines Product Claim Scope, Not System
items.summary: A recent decision from the Düsseldorf Local Division in UPC_CFI_351/2024 provides an interesting look at the Unified Patent Court's (UPC) evolving approach to claim construction and the role...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方法院在UPC_CFI_351/2024案中裁定，描述为“可拆卸安装”的打印机供墨容器属于产品权利要求而非系统权利要求。法院认定“可安装”仅界定产品适用性，不要求外部设备存在。因此，被告单独销售的墨盒构成直接侵权。该判决明确了功能性语言在权利要求解释中的作用，区分了产品与系统权利要求的边界，对后续类似案件的侵权判定具有指导意义。
items.ai_summary_en: In case UPC_CFI_351/2024, the Unified Patent Court’s Düsseldorf Local Division clarified the interpretation of functional language in patent claims. The court ruled that a printer supply container described as 'detachably mountable' constitutes a product claim rather than a system claim. The judgment established that terms like 'mountable' define the product's suitability for intended use, without requiring the physical presence of the external apparatus, such as the printer itself. Consequently, the defendant’s standalone toner bottles were found to directly infringe the patent. This decision highlights the UPC’s evolving approach to distinguishing between product and system claim boundaries, emphasizing that functional limitations do not necessarily expand claim scope to include the entire system environment.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业需警惕单一零部件在UPC被认定为直接侵权的风险。在撰写欧洲专利时，应审慎使用“可安装”等功能性限定，避免将产品权利要求不当扩大至系统层面。建议针对核心组件进行独立权利要求布局，并在出口前进行FTO分析，评估单独销售行为在UPC体系下的侵权可能性，以规避高额赔偿风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling significantly impacts how functional limitations are interpreted in European patent litigation. Parties should recognize that describing a component as 'mountable' or 'capable of supporting' does not automatically convert a product claim into a system claim requiring the full assembly for infringement. For rights holders, this strengthens protection for standalone spare parts and consumables. Conversely, manufacturers of individual components must conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses, considering that their products may infringe even when sold separately from the main device. Careful claim drafting is essential to avoid unintended broadening of scope through functional terminology.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院裁定“可安装”界定产品适用性，无需外部设备存在即构成侵权", "明确区分产品权利要求与系统权利要求，单独销售组件可直接侵权", "确立UPC在功能性语言解释上的倾向，强化专利权人对配套产品的保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court ruled that 'mountable' defines product suitability for intended use, not requiring the external apparatus to be present.", "A clear distinction was drawn between product and system claims, establishing that standalone components can constitute direct infringement.", "The decision confirms the UPC's tendency to interpret functional language as defining product capabilities rather than system dependencies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕单一零部件在UPC被认定为直接侵权的法律风险", "优化欧洲专利权利要求布局，审慎使用功能性限定语言"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Standalone components may face direct infringement liability under product claims using functional language.", "Practice note: Drafters should carefully evaluate functional terms to prevent unintended expansion from product to system claim scope.", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions regarding the boundary between product suitability definitions and system-level requirements."]
core_points_en: The court ruled that 'mountable' defines product suitability for intended use, not requiring the external apparatus to be present.
A clear distinction was drawn between product and system claims, establishing that standalone components can constitute direct infringement.
The decision confirms the UPC's tendency to interpret functional language as defining product capabilities rather than system dependencies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Standalone components may face direct infringement liability under product claims using functional language.
Practice note: Drafters should carefully evaluate functional terms to prevent unintended expansion from product to system claim scope.
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions regarding the boundary between product suitability definitions and system-level requirements.

items.title: UPC: Supplementing Expert Opinion After Inspection Not Possible
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分部裁定：检查后不得补充专家意见
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Denies Supplementary Expert Opinion Following Ex Parte Evidence Preservation
items.summary: The Düsseldorf local division of the Unified Patent Court has ruled on a defendant's request for a supplementary expert opinion following an ex-parte inspection order.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在UPC_CFI_1325/2025案中裁定，驳回被告要求法院委任专家就单方证据保全出具补充意见的请求。法院指出，被告可通过《程序规则》第197.3条申请审查以保障程序权利，且该专家非第187条规定的“法院专家”，无法律依据支持补充报告。证据保全旨在快速获取信息以决定是否起诉，实质准确性异议应在后续主程序中提出。
items.ai_summary_en: The Düsseldorf Local Division of the Unified Patent Court dismissed a defendant’s request for a supplementary expert opinion following an ex parte inspection order in case UPC_CFI_1325/2025. The court held that no legal basis existed for such an order, distinguishing the court-commissioned inspector from a formal 'court expert' under Rule 187 of the Rules of Procedure. Consequently, parties lack the right to comment on the initial report. The panel emphasized that evidence preservation aims to rapidly gather information to decide on litigation, with substantive accuracy challenges reserved for main proceedings. Procedural safeguards were deemed sufficient via Rule 197.3 review applications. While rejecting the supplementary report, the court noted specific technical queries regarding the expert’s sketches and access to protective letters, signaling these issues will likely resurface during subsequent main actions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇单方证据保全时，应聚焦于利用Rule 197.3申请审查而非纠缠补充报告。需重视保全阶段的策略应对，因实质抗辩将延至主程序。建议提前准备技术对比分析，确保在主程序中有效反驳专家初步结论，避免程序被动。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties subject to ex parte evidence preservation should prioritize procedural reviews under Rule 197.3 rather than seeking supplementary reports, which are unlikely to succeed. Since substantive technical disputes are deferred to main proceedings, immediate strategy should focus on preserving rights for later argumentation. Legal teams must meticulously prepare detailed technical counter-analyses and ensure all relevant documentation, such as protective letters, is formally recorded. This approach ensures effective rebuttal of preliminary expert findings when the case moves to the substantive phase, avoiding procedural delays while maintaining strong defensive positions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["驳回单方保全后补充专家意见请求，明确缺乏法律依据", "区分法院委任专家与法定法院专家，排除当事人评论权", "保全程序侧重效率，实体争议留待主程序解决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Request for supplementary expert opinion after ex parte inspection denied due to lack of legal basis.", "Distinction made between court-commissioned inspectors and formal court experts under Rule 187 RoP.", "Substantive objections to evidence accuracy must be raised in main proceedings rather than preservation stage."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["善用Rule 197.3申请审查替代补充专家意见诉求", "将实质性技术抗辩保留至主程序阶段集中发力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Focus on Rule 197.3 review mechanisms instead of pursuing supplementary expert opinions post-inspection.", "Practice note: Defer substantive technical defenses to main proceedings; use preservation phase strictly for procedural compliance.", "What to monitor: How courts handle technical discrepancies and document accessibility questions raised during preservation in future main actions."]
core_points_en: Request for supplementary expert opinion after ex parte inspection denied due to lack of legal basis.
Distinction made between court-commissioned inspectors and formal court experts under Rule 187 RoP.
Substantive objections to evidence accuracy must be raised in main proceedings rather than preservation stage.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Focus on Rule 197.3 review mechanisms instead of pursuing supplementary expert opinions post-inspection.
Practice note: Defer substantive technical defenses to main proceedings; use preservation phase strictly for procedural compliance.
What to monitor: How courts handle technical discrepancies and document accessibility questions raised during preservation in future main actions.

items.title: UPC Ruling Defines The Limits Of Unitary Patent Protection
items.title_zh: UPC 裁决界定单一专利保护的适用极限
items.title_en: UPC Appeal Court Confirms Strict Territorial Scope for Unitary Patent Effect
items.summary: The Unified Patent Court system offers streamlined patent protection across multiple European jurisdictions, but understanding its mechanisms and strategic implications remains crucial for rights holders.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉院确认，欧洲专利若要在所有参与成员国获得单一效力，必须在各国拥有完全相同的权利要求集。因马耳他于申请日后才加入欧洲专利公约，导致涉案专利无法覆盖该国，故被驳回单一效力请求。法院坚持严格解释法规，拒绝因地域缺失非权利人可控而给予灵活性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has clarified the strict territorial requirements for unitary patent protection. The ruling confirms that a European patent benefits from unitary effect only if it covers all participating member states with an identical set of claims in each jurisdiction. In this specific case, the patent proprietor sought unitary effect for a divisional application filed in 2005. However, Malta, a current participating state, had not yet acceded to the European Patent Convention at the time of filing. Consequently, the patent did not cover Malta. The Court rejected arguments for flexibility regarding circumstances beyond the proprietor's control, upholding the European Patent Office’s refusal to grant unitary effect due to the lack of uniform territorial coverage across all participating states.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请专利时，需严格核查目标国在优先权日或申请日的公约成员状态。对于涉及新加入成员国的分案申请，应评估单一专利路径的可行性，避免因部分国家缺失导致整体单一效力失效，必要时考虑传统生效模式。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders must verify the European Patent Convention membership status of all participating states as of the priority or filing date when seeking unitary effect. For divisional applications involving countries that joined the system after the original filing, the absence of coverage in those jurisdictions will preclude unitary protection entirely. Strategic planning should assess whether to pursue traditional national validations for specific gaps or accept the loss of unitary status. This strict interpretation emphasizes the need for early due diligence on territorial coverage to avoid unexpected limitations in enforcement scope and cost-saving benefits associated with the unitary patent system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["单一效力要求专利在所有参与国具有完全相同的权利要求集", "申请日前未加入公约的成员国（如马耳他）导致无法获得单一保护", "UPC 上诉院拒绝因客观情况排除领土而放宽单一效力的法定条件"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Unitary effect requires identical claims covering all participating member states without exception or territorial gaps.", "Patents filed before a country's accession to the EPC cannot include that state, disqualifying them from unitary protection.", "The UPC Court of Appeal rejects flexible interpretations, strictly enforcing statutory conditions despite uncontrollable historical factors."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["核查申请日各国公约状态，避免因地域缺失丧失单一效力", "针对特定国家缺口，提前规划传统验证与单一专利的组合策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify EPC membership status of all participating states at the filing date to ensure eligibility for unitary effect.", "Practice note: Evaluate alternative strategies, such as traditional national validations, for divisional cases with unavoidable territorial gaps.", "What to monitor: Future UPC jurisprudence regarding strict adherence to territorial uniformity and potential legislative adjustments."]
core_points_en: Unitary effect requires identical claims covering all participating member states without exception or territorial gaps.
Patents filed before a country's accession to the EPC cannot include that state, disqualifying them from unitary protection.
The UPC Court of Appeal rejects flexible interpretations, strictly enforcing statutory conditions despite uncontrollable historical factors.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Verify EPC membership status of all participating states at the filing date to ensure eligibility for unitary effect.
Practice note: Evaluate alternative strategies, such as traditional national validations, for divisional cases with unavoidable territorial gaps.
What to monitor: Future UPC jurisprudence regarding strict adherence to territorial uniformity and potential legislative adjustments.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 27, 2026, Decision, UPC_CoA_409/2025, UPC_CoA_410/2025, UPC_CoA_420/2025
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院裁定登录系统不构成应诉管辖
items.title_en: UPC Appeal Court Rules System Login Does Not Constitute Appearance Under Brussels Ia
items.summary: Logging into the UPC's Case Management System does not constitute an "entering of an appearance" to establish jurisdiction under the Brussels Ia Regulation.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在涉及 NUC Electronics 与 Hurom 的案件中裁定，被告仅登录案件管理系统查看文件，不构成《布鲁塞尔条例 Ia》意义上的“出庭应诉”。法院指出，确立管辖权需被告采取实质性步骤，如提交初步异议或答辩状，单纯访问不足以视为对法院管辖权的自愿接受或争议确认。
items.ai_summary_en: In a decision dated March 27, 2026, the Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal clarified that merely logging into the Case Management System to view case files does not amount to an 'entering of an appearance' under Article 26(1) of the Brussels Ia Regulation. The Court held that passive access to documents, prior to any active procedural step or defense, is insufficient to demonstrate a deliberate choice regarding the UPC's jurisdiction. To establish jurisdiction through appearance, a defendant must take substantive action, such as filing a Preliminary Objection under Rule 19 of the Rules of Procedure or submitting a Statement of Defence on the merits. This ruling ensures that jurisdiction is not inadvertently established through routine administrative access, requiring instead a clear, intentional engagement with the court's authority.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对 UPC 诉讼时，切勿因登录查看案情而误判为已应诉。应严格把控首次提交文件的时机与内容，避免因程序性操作不当导致丧失管辖权异议权利，需由专业律师评估后采取行动。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment provides critical clarity for parties involved in UPC proceedings regarding the threshold for establishing jurisdiction via appearance. Legal teams can safely access case materials via the system for preliminary assessment without risking an inadvertent waiver of jurisdictional objections. However, caution is required when preparing the first substantive submission. Parties should ensure that any initial filing explicitly addresses jurisdictional issues if they intend to challenge the court's authority, rather than assuming that mere system interaction preserves their rights. Strategic timing and content of the first formal response remain decisive.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仅登录 UPC 系统查看文件不视为法律意义上的出庭应诉行为", "确立管辖权需提交初步异议或实体答辩等实质性诉讼步骤", "明确区分被动查阅与主动抗辩，防止管辖权被意外确立"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Logging into the UPC Case Management System to view files does not constitute entering an appearance under the Brussels Ia Regulation.", "Establishing jurisdiction requires substantive steps, such as filing a Preliminary Objection or a Statement of Defence on the merits.", "The decision distinguishes between passive file access and active litigation conduct to prevent accidental acceptance of jurisdiction."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["登录系统查阅卷宗不会触发管辖权，企业可安全进行初步评估", "首次提交异议或答辩状是确立管辖的关键节点，须谨慎决策"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Passive system access for case evaluation does not trigger jurisdictional acceptance, allowing safe preliminary review.", "Practice note: Ensure the first substantive filing clearly articulates jurisdictional challenges to preserve objection rights.", "What to monitor: Future interpretations of what constitutes an 'active step' beyond standard filings under Rule 19 and Rule 23."]
core_points_en: Logging into the UPC Case Management System to view files does not constitute entering an appearance under the Brussels Ia Regulation.
Establishing jurisdiction requires substantive steps, such as filing a Preliminary Objection or a Statement of Defence on the merits.
The decision distinguishes between passive file access and active litigation conduct to prevent accidental acceptance of jurisdiction.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Passive system access for case evaluation does not trigger jurisdictional acceptance, allowing safe preliminary review.
Practice note: Ensure the first substantive filing clearly articulates jurisdictional challenges to preserve objection rights.
What to monitor: Future interpretations of what constitutes an 'active step' beyond standard filings under Rule 19 and Rule 23.

items.title: Düsseldorf LD, March 18, 2026, Decision On Infringement Action And Counterclaim For Revocation, UPC_CFI_135/2024, UPC_CFI_477/2024
items.title_zh: UPC 杜塞尔多夫分院就 Dolby 诉 Beko 案作出侵权与无效反诉判决
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Dominant Position in FRAND and Implementer Willingness in Dolby v Beko
items.summary: The Düsseldorf Local Division held that a dominant position within the meaning of Article 102 TFEU may arise if smart TVs complying with a common standard...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方法院在 Dolby 诉 Beko 案中裁定，若符合标准的智能电视无许可无法上市，专利权人可能拥有市场支配地位。法院强调，若实施者未在 FRAND 谈判初期表明许可意愿，则无需审查专利权人报价是否合规。该判决遵循华为诉中兴案框架，并与曼海姆分院立场保持一致。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Düsseldorf Local Division ruled that a dominant position under Article 102 TFEU may exist if standard-compliant smart TVs cannot be marketed without a license for the patent-in-suit. The court emphasized that consumer expectations regarding common audio and video codecs are decisive. Furthermore, the judgment clarifies that if an implementer fails to demonstrate willingness to conclude a license during the initial stages of FRAND negotiations, the patentee’s offer need not be examined for compliance. This decision aligns with the Huawei v ZTE framework and maintains consistency with the Mannheim Local Division’s approach in Panasonic v Oppo, reinforcing procedural strictness in FRAND defense strategies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧出口智能电视等标准产品时，须在收到侵权通知后立即明确表达许可意愿，否则将丧失 FRAND 抗辩机会。建议提前建立标准化应对流程，避免因程序瑕疵导致败诉风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Implementers of standard-essential patents must explicitly express willingness to negotiate a license immediately upon receiving an infringement notice. Failure to do so at the preliminary stage precludes further review of the patentee’s licensing terms, effectively waiving FRAND defenses. Companies should establish standardized internal protocols to ensure timely, written responses to infringement claims. Proactive risk assessment and prepared negotiation strategies are essential to avoid procedural defaults that could lead to unfavorable injunctions or damages in European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标准合规电视若无许可无法销售，专利权人或具市场支配地位", "实施者未表许可意愿时，无需审查专利权人报价是否符合 FRAND", "判决遵循华为诉中兴框架，确认 UPC 内部裁判标准一致性"]

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 11, 2026, Decision, UPC_CFI_934/2025
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院裁定撤诉规则适用于临时措施申请
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Confirms Rule 265 Withdrawal Applies to Provisional Measures
items.summary: The Court of Appeal clarified that the term "action" (Klage) in Rule 265.1 RoP encompasses applications for provisional measures.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在 2026 年 3 月 11 日判决中澄清，程序规则第 265 条中的“诉讼”一词涵盖临时措施申请。法院确认该撤诉机制不仅适用于侵权或无效等主诉程序，同样延伸至临时救济程序及上诉阶段。若双方达成和解且无异议，法院可准许撤诉并免除单独的费用裁决，确立了更灵活的程序退出机制。
items.ai_summary_en: In a March 11, 2026 decision, the Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal clarified that the term 'action' in Rule 265.1 of the Rules of Procedure encompasses applications for provisional measures. The Court confirmed that the withdrawal mechanism extends beyond main infringement or revocation proceedings to include interim relief proceedings and remains applicable during appeal stages. Withdrawal is permitted before a final appellate decision is rendered, provided no legitimate interests of the opposing party oppose it. In this case, the applicants withdrew their provisional measure application during the pending appeal after reaching an out-of-court settlement. The Court further held that a separate cost decision under Rule 265.2 RoP is unnecessary when parties unanimously agree to bear their own costs, establishing a more flexible procedural exit mechanism for litigants.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应对专利纠纷时，可利用此规则在上诉阶段灵活撤回临时措施申请以促成和解。建议密切关注 UPC 程序动态，在面临禁令风险时，将程序性撤诉作为谈判筹码，优化诉讼成本与商业策略的平衡。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling provides litigants with greater procedural flexibility in UPC proceedings involving interim relief. Parties seeking to settle disputes can utilize the withdrawal mechanism at the appeal stage to facilitate commercial resolutions without waiting for a final judgment on the merits. By explicitly confirming that mutual agreement on cost bearing eliminates the need for a formal cost decision, the Court streamlines the conclusion of cases. This approach allows companies to optimize litigation strategies by balancing procedural concessions with business objectives, potentially reducing both time and economic burdens associated with prolonged patent enforcement actions in Europe.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["程序规则第 265 条明确适用于临时措施申请，不限于狭义的主诉行动", "上诉阶段允许撤回临时措施申请，前提是终审判决作出前提出且无损对方利益", "双方一致同意各自承担费用时，法院无需再就费用问题作出单独裁决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 265 RoP applies to provisional measure applications, not limited to narrow definitions of main actions.", "Withdrawal of provisional measures is permitted at the appeal stage if declared before the final decision.", "No separate cost decision is required if parties unanimously agree each side bears its own costs."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用撤诉规则灵活性，在上诉阶段通过程序退出让步换取商业和解空间", "明确费用分担共识可简化结案流程，降低企业在欧诉讼的时间与经济成本"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage withdrawal rules in appeals to create space for commercial settlements and manage injunction risks.", "Practice note: Secure unanimous agreement on cost allocation to simplify case closure and avoid separate cost rulings.", "What to monitor: UPC procedural dynamics regarding the scope of 'legitimate interests' opposing withdrawal in future cases."]

items.title: Court Of Appeal, March 6, 2026, Decision, UPC_COA_895/2025 & UPC_COA_896/2025
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭裁定：撤诉许可及诉讼费退还新规适用
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Withdrawal Permitted with Consent; Fee Reimbursement Rules Clarified
items.summary: Under R. 265.1 RoP, a party may withdraw its action as long as no final decision has been rendered. The withdrawal shall not be permitted if the other party...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在 2026 年 3 月裁决中确认，若对方无异议且无合法利益，上诉人可依据程序规则第 265 条撤回上诉。同时，法院明确 2026 年生效的规则修订版将诉讼费退还比例从 60% 降至 50%，适用版本以申请退还日期为准。此外，若双方未请求，法院可在撤诉时不作出费用决定。
items.ai_summary_en: In a March 2026 decision, the Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal confirmed that an appellant may withdraw an appeal under Rule 265 of the Rules of Procedure if the respondent consents and lacks a legitimate interest in continuing the proceedings. The Court clarified that this rule applies equally to appellate stages. Regarding costs, the Court applied the amended Rule 370.9, effective January 1, 2026, which reduced fee reimbursement from 60% to 50%. The applicable version is determined by the date the reimbursement application is filed, not when the appeal was lodged. Consequently, the appellant received only 50% reimbursement. Additionally, although Rule 265.2(c) provides for a cost decision upon withdrawal, the Court declined to issue one as neither party requested it at the appellate stage.
items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners must carefully assess the timing of withdrawal applications to navigate the reduced fee reimbursement rates under the updated Rules of Procedure. Since the reimbursement percentage is tied to the application date rather than the appeal filing date, strategic planning is essential to optimize cost recovery. Furthermore, parties should actively request specific cost decisions if they seek clarity or enforcement regarding expense allocation, as the Court will not automatically issue such rulings upon withdrawal without a formal request. This highlights the importance of precise procedural management in UPC litigation.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["Withdrawal of appeal is permitted under Rule 265 RoP when the respondent consents and has no legitimate interest in the action being decided.", "Amended Rule 370.9 reduces fee reimbursement to 50%; the applicable version depends on the date the reimbursement application is filed.", "The Court of Appeal may refrain from issuing a cost decision upon withdrawal if neither party explicitly requests such a determination."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate the financial impact of reduced fee reimbursements when deciding whether to withdraw appeals after January 1, 2026.", "Practice note: File reimbursement applications strategically to align with favorable procedural rules and explicitly request cost decisions if needed.", "What to monitor: Ensure respondent consent is obtained for withdrawals and verify the absence of legitimate interest to avoid procedural obstacles."]
core_points_en: Withdrawal of appeal is permitted under Rule 265 RoP when the respondent consents and has no legitimate interest in the action being decided.
Amended Rule 370.9 reduces fee reimbursement to 50%; the applicable version depends on the date the reimbursement application is filed.
The Court of Appeal may refrain from issuing a cost decision upon withdrawal if neither party explicitly requests such a determination.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate the financial impact of reduced fee reimbursements when deciding whether to withdraw appeals after January 1, 2026.
Practice note: File reimbursement applications strategically to align with favorable procedural rules and explicitly request cost decisions if needed.
What to monitor: Ensure respondent consent is obtained for withdrawals and verify the absence of legitimate interest to avoid procedural obstacles.

items.summary: Mediation is an increasingly attractive option to resolve IP disputes, especially for small and medium-sized enterprises, individuals and universities
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出调解因高效、保密及商业导向优势，成为解决IP纠纷的优选。WIPO数据显示2025年案件激增42%，中小企业占比高。欧盟层面，EUIPO扩大调解服务范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解中心。调解由中立专家主持，灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供法院无法给予的商业解决方案，有助于建立双赢局面。

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 12, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1747/2025
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院裁定：临时禁令需证明单一产品侵权
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Dismisses Preliminary Injunction: Strict Single-Product Infringement Proof Required
items.summary: The applicant bears the burden of proof for infringement in provisional measures and must demonstrate with sufficient certainty that infringement is more likely than not (Art. 62 UPCA, R. 211 RoP)
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在2026年5月12日裁决中驳回申请人临时禁令请求。法院强调，申请人须充分证明侵权行为发生的可能性大于不可能性。针对隐形牙套专利，法院认定所有技术特征必须体现在单个产品中，不同阶段的牙套不能组合认定侵权。因申请人仅依赖宣传材料且缺乏具体技术分析，未能满足举证标准，法院未审理有效性等其他抗辩理由。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf dismissed an applicant’s request for preliminary injunctions on May 12, 2026. The Court emphasized that applicants must demonstrate with sufficient certainty that infringement is more likely than not. Regarding clear aligner patents, the ruling established that all technical features of a method claim must be embodied within a single product. Consequently, different treatment phases executed by separate aligners cannot be combined to establish infringement. Since the applicant relied exclusively on promotional materials without providing specific technical analysis or independent assessment of the accused devices, they failed to meet the required standard of proof. As a result, the Court did not examine other defenses, such as validity challenges, highlighting the critical importance of robust technical evidence in provisional proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国医疗器械企业出口欧洲时，应警惕UPC对‘单一产品’特征的严格解释。在应对临时禁令指控时，需准备详尽的技术比对分析，避免仅依赖营销材料。建议提前进行FTO分析，确保多阶段治疗方案不落入同一专利的单一产品权利要求范围，以规避高额诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the UPC’s strict interpretation of 'single product' requirements for multi-stage medical treatments like clear aligner therapy. Parties seeking or defending against preliminary injunctions must prepare detailed, feature-by-feature technical comparisons rather than relying on marketing collateral. For companies operating in Europe, this highlights the necessity of conducting thorough Freedom-to-Operate analyses early in product development. Ensuring that multi-stage treatment protocols do not inadvertently fall within the scope of a single-product patent claim is essential to mitigate litigation risks and avoid costly enforcement actions at the Unified Patent Court.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["申请人承担临时禁令侵权举证责任，需证明侵权可能性大于不可能性", "方法权利要求的所有特征必须在单个对齐器中实现，不可跨阶段组合", "仅凭宣传材料和推测性证言不足以确立复杂技术领域的侵权事实"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Applicants bear the burden of proof in provisional measures, requiring demonstration that infringement is more likely than not.", "All method claim features must be realized within a single aligner; features across different treatment stages cannot be aggregated.", "Promotional materials and speculative witness statements are insufficient to prove infringement in complex technical fields."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视UPC对单一产品特征比对的严格要求，避免跨阶段组合侵权风险", "完善技术证据链，避免仅凭营销材料应对复杂的专利侵权指控"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strict adherence to single-product infringement definitions is critical for multi-stage medical device strategies.", "Practice note: Develop comprehensive technical evidence chains avoiding reliance on general marketing materials for legal defense.", "What to monitor: UPC case law regarding the aggregation of features across distinct treatment phases in method claims."]
core_points_en: Applicants bear the burden of proof in provisional measures, requiring demonstration that infringement is more likely than not.
All method claim features must be realized within a single aligner; features across different treatment stages cannot be aggregated.
Promotional materials and speculative witness statements are insufficient to prove infringement in complex technical fields.

Practice note: Develop comprehensive technical evidence chains avoiding reliance on general marketing materials for legal defense.
What to monitor: UPC case law regarding the aggregation of features across distinct treatment phases in method claims.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, April 30, 2026, Order Rejecting Appeal As Inadmissible, UPC_CoA_1/2026
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院裁定驳回上诉：程序合规与费用担保标准
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Rejects Adobe Appeal: Strict Procedural Compliance and Cost Security Standards
items.summary: The Court of Appeal verifies ex officio compliance with the time limits and rules governing the appeal. When deciding on the admissibility of an appeal, an order rejecting the appeal as...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在Adobe诉Keeex案中，裁定驳回上诉并确认程序规则需严格适用以保障法律确定性。法院依职权审查上诉期限，认定驳回裁定未超出请求范围。此外，法院重申原告与被告的相对财务状况不构成Rule 158下的费用担保考量因素。该判决强化了UPC程序规则的刚性，强调避免司法任意性，对后续涉及费用担保及程序合规的案件具有先例约束力。
items.ai_summary_en: In the case of Adobe Inc. v. Keeex SAS, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal dismissed the appeal, reinforcing the strict application of procedural rules to ensure legal certainty. The Court conducted an ex officio review of appeal deadlines, confirming that a rejection order for inadmissibility does not exceed the scope of the claims. Furthermore, the judgment reaffirmed established case law stating that the relative financial disparity between claimant and defendant is not a valid criterion under Rule 158 of the Rules of Procedure for determining cost security. This decision underscores the UPC’s commitment to preventing arbitrary judicial treatment and highlights the binding nature of procedural compliance in future litigation involving cost security and admissibility challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，须严守程序期限，避免因技术性违规导致权利丧失。针对费用担保，不能仅凭双方财力差距抗辩，需准备实质性的胜诉可能性证据。建议加强内部IP合规流程，特别是跨境诉讼中的程序管理，以降低因程序失误带来的高额成本风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Litigants before the UPC must prioritize rigorous adherence to procedural timelines and formal requirements, as technical non-compliance can lead to immediate dismissal regardless of substantive merits. Regarding cost security, arguments based solely on financial disparity are unlikely to succeed; instead, parties should focus on demonstrating a strong prima facie case regarding the likelihood of success on the merits. Legal teams should strengthen internal cross-border litigation protocols to manage procedural risks effectively. Monitoring how the Court applies these strict standards in subsequent cases will be crucial for developing robust defense strategies and minimizing exposure to high procedural costs.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院依职权严格审查上诉期限与程序规则，确保法律确定性及非歧视原则", "驳回上诉作为不可受理裁定，不视为超出当事人诉讼请求的范围", "当事人相对财务状况不构成申请费用担保的法定依据，维持既往判例立场"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严守UPC程序期限，规避因技术性违规导致的权利丧失风险", "费用担保抗辩需侧重胜诉可能性，而非单纯依赖财力对比"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure strict adherence to UPC procedural deadlines to avoid rights forfeiture due to technical violations.", "Practice note: Substantiate cost security defenses with evidence of merit rather than relying on comparative financial strength.", "What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 158 and ex officio admissibility checks in UPC cost security proceedings."]
core_points_en: The Court strictly reviews appeal deadlines and procedural rules ex officio to uphold legal certainty and prevent discriminatory or arbitrary judicial administration.
An order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible is legally distinct and does not constitute a decision exceeding the scope of the parties' specific claims.

Practice note: Substantiate cost security defenses with evidence of merit rather than relying on comparative financial strength.
What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 158 and ex officio admissibility checks in UPC cost security proceedings.

items.title: Central Division Paris accepts revocation action against expired patent
items.title_zh: UPC巴黎分部：过期专利撤销之诉若具法律利益仍属可受理
items.title_en: UPC Paris: Revocation Action Admissible for Expired Patent if Legal Interest Persists
items.summary: Huntsman (Europe) BV and Huntsman Holland BV v BASF SE — Central Division Paris, 22 April 2026 (UPC_CFI_461/2025))
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC巴黎中央分部在Huntsman诉BASF案中裁定，即使涉案专利已过期，只要原告因追索过往侵权损害赔偿而保留合法法律利益，撤销诉讼即具可受理性。法院重申在创造性评估中不受专利自身技术问题表述限制，可独立界定客观技术问题。此外，辅助请求的审查仅限于针对修改后权利要求的具体无效攻击，不自动沿用对授权权利要求的异议。
items.ai_summary_en: In Huntsman v BASF, the UPC Central Division Paris ruled that a revocation action remains admissible even after patent expiry, provided the claimant retains a legitimate legal interest, such as pursuing damages for past infringement. The Court confirmed it is not bound by the objective technical problem stated in the patent specification and may independently define this problem during inventive-step analysis. Furthermore, regarding auxiliary requests, the Court established that only specific invalidity attacks directed at amended claims are considered. Objections raised against granted claims do not automatically carry over to modified versions, requiring parties to precisely target their arguments in amendment proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕UPC程序灵活性，即便专利过期，对手仍可借撤销之诉清除障碍以索赔。应对时须精准论证法律利益缺失，并在答复辅助请求时聚焦具体修改点，避免异议范围不当扩大，强化程序抗辩策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights the procedural flexibility of the UPC, where expired patents can still be challenged to clear obstacles for subsequent damages claims. Parties must rigorously assess whether a plaintiff retains a legitimate legal interest to block such actions early. In response strategies, defendants should carefully tailor their defenses for auxiliary requests, focusing strictly on the specific amendments made. This prevents the unintended expansion of invalidity grounds and ensures that objections to original claims do not automatically prejudice modified versions, thereby strengthening procedural defense positions.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕过期专利撤销风险，积极抗辩原告缺乏合法法律利益以阻断诉讼", "优化辅助请求答复策略，严格限定审查范围，防止无效理由不当扩展"]

Courts may independently determine the objective technical problem and are not confined to the wording used in the patent specification.

items.title: LD Helsinki, April 29, 2026, Order Disposing Of Provisional Measures Application, ACT_551054/2023
items.title_zh: UPC赫尔辛基地院：临时措施申请失去目的时予以处置
items.title_en: UPC Helsinki: Disposing Provisional Measures When Devoid of Purpose Under Rule 360
items.summary: If a provisional measures application has become devoid of purpose and hence there is no longer need to adjudicate on it, the Court shall dispose of it based on R. 360 RoP...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月29日，UPC赫尔辛基地方法院就ACT_551054/2023案发布命令。法院依据《程序规则》第360条裁定，若临时措施申请已失去目的且无需再行裁决，应予以处置。若当事人请求，法院可作出费用决定，并酌情考虑实体案件已知结果。本案涉及AIM Sport Development AG与多家TGI Sport实体关于EP 3 295 663号专利的争议。
items.ai_summary_en: On April 29, 2026, the UPC Helsinki Local Division issued an order in case ACT_551054/2023 regarding European Patent EP 3 295 663. The Court applied Rule 360 of the Rules of Procedure to dispose of an application for provisional measures that had become devoid of purpose, determining that further adjudication was unnecessary. The dispute involved applicant AIM Sport Development AG and multiple respondents within the TGI Sport group. Crucially, the ruling clarifies that upon request by the parties, the Court may issue a cost decision. In making this determination, the Court retains the discretion to consider the known outcome of the main proceedings on the merits. This procedural guidance highlights the mechanism for terminating interim relief proceedings when the underlying necessity ceases, while allowing for equitable allocation of legal costs based on the substantive case resolution.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC临时禁令时，需密切关注程序进展。若主案胜诉或和解，应及时主张临时措施失去目的以终止程序，避免不必要的费用支出。建议结合实体判决结果，积极向法院申请费用分担，优化跨境知识产权诉讼的成本控制策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision provides critical procedural clarity for litigants before the Unified Patent Court. When a main action is resolved through settlement or final judgment, interim relief applications often lose their practical utility. Parties should proactively seek dismissal of such measures under Rule 360 to avoid unnecessary procedural steps. Furthermore, requesting a cost decision allows courts to factor in the substantive case outcome, potentially leading to more favorable cost allocations. Strategic management of these procedural transitions is essential for optimizing litigation budgets and minimizing exposure to adverse cost orders in cross-border patent disputes.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, Standing Judge, April 28, 2026, Order On Request For Discretionary Review, UPC_CoA_56/2026
items.title_zh: UPC上诉庭裁定：错过期限的人为错误可获追溯延期
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Human Error Under Systemic Checks Warrants Retroactive Term Extension
items.summary: The Court of Appeal examines a request for discretionary review in a patent dispute between Optopol Technology and Topcon Corporation concerning EP 2 845 534. The case involves interpretation of procedural rules governing standing and review processes under the Unified Patent Court framework.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本案系Optopol与Topcon在统一专利法院（UPC）的专利纠纷。上诉庭审查了针对EP 2845534号专利的程序性请求。法院认定，尽管代理人助理系统录入失误导致错过期限，但律所已建立核查机制，该人为错误属可原谅情形。上诉庭支持地方法院决定，允许在权利恢复请求期内隐含提交追溯期限延长申请，强调此类申请应尽早提出，但在特定未决判例背景下具有合理性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal examined a request for discretionary review in patent litigation between Optopol Technology and Topcon Corporation concerning EP 2 845 534. The dispute centered on procedural rules governing standing and the re-establishment of rights. The Local Division had found that a missed deadline resulted from an excusable human error, specifically an assistant’s incorrect data entry, despite the law firm having established systemic checks. The Court of Appeal upheld this determination as reasonable. It further validated the implicit filing of a retroactive term extension request under Rule 9.3 RoP within the timeframe for re-establishment of rights. While emphasizing that such requests should generally be made promptly, the Court acknowledged the reasonableness of the timing given the pending precedent in Angelalign v. Angel Technology at that stage.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需建立严格的期限双重核查机制以防范人为失误。若遇不可抗力或合理疏忽，应及时利用“可原谅错误”条款申请救济。同时，注意程序请求的时效性与独立性，避免过度依赖替代性请求策略，确保合规操作以降低专利失效风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the threshold for excusable error under the Unified Patent Court framework, balancing strict procedural compliance with practical realities. Parties should ensure their docketing systems include redundant verification layers to demonstrate due care when relying on human error defenses. While alternative pleading strategies, such as implicitly combining term extension requests with re-establishment applications, may be accepted, practitioners must prioritize promptness. Monitoring evolving case law regarding procedural discretion remains essential to navigate complex timelines effectively and mitigate risks of patent lapse due to administrative oversights.

items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地院2026年4月程序令：关于提交文件可采性的裁决
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Order: Admissibility of Late Rejoinder Arguments on Amendment Motion
items.summary: The admissibility of the documents and arguments submitted is a question of fact. Whether the defendant’s submissions in its rejoinder regarding the motion to amend are relevant...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方分院发布程序令（案号UPC_CFI_869/2025），涉及大日本印刷诉Zapp等公司专利侵权案。法院裁定，被告在反驳中针对修改动议提交的论据是否相关及是否逾期，属事实问题，需待口头程序结束后方能确定。依据武器平等原则，法院允许原告就被告的事实陈述补充评论，作为预防性措施，且不 prejudge 最终的可采性决定。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Düsseldorf Local Division issued a procedural order in case UPC_CFI_869/2025, involving Dai Nippon Printing Co., Ltd. against Zapp AG and Zapp Precision Metals GmbH. The court addressed the admissibility of documents and arguments submitted by the defendant in its rejoinder regarding a motion to amend. It determined that assessing whether these submissions are relevant or late is a factual question requiring thorough analysis, which can only be conclusively resolved at the end of oral proceedings. To uphold the principle of equality of arms, the court granted the plaintiff an additional opportunity to comment on the defendant’s statement of facts. This measure is precautionary and without prejudice to the final decision on whether the defendant’s additional arguments will be admitted into the record.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，应高度重视程序规则的灵活性。即便对方提交材料可能逾期，法院仍可能基于公平原则给予我方回应机会。建议企业建立快速响应机制，充分利用程序赋予的评论权，同时密切关注法官对“武器平等”原则的适用尺度，以优化诉讼策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This procedural order highlights the UPC’s flexible approach to managing late submissions and amendment motions. Parties should recognize that courts may prioritize procedural fairness over strict deadlines when balancing the interests of both sides. Litigants must remain agile, prepared to utilize opportunities for supplementary comments effectively. Establishing rapid response mechanisms allows teams to address unexpected factual assertions promptly. Furthermore, monitoring how judges apply the 'equality of arms' principle provides strategic insights into evidentiary standards. Understanding this judicial discretion helps optimize litigation strategies, ensuring that procedural rights are fully leveraged to counter potential surprises from opposing counsel during complex patent disputes.
